input.question: Which courses have the highest failure rate?

payload.question: Which courses have the highest failure rate?

output.reasoning: Discovered relevant tables (courses, course_sections, enrollments, grades) and computed per-course failure counts and rates. I define failure as a numeric grade < 3.0 or common failing letter codes, and compute rate among enrollments that have a recorded grade. Results ordered deterministically by failure_rate desc then course_code.
output.sql:
WITH course_stats AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.code AS course_code,
    c.name AS course_name,
    COUNT(g.id) FILTER (WHERE g.id IS NOT NULL) AS total_graded,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN (
               g.grade_value ~ '^[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?$'
               AND g.grade_value::numeric < 3.0
             )
             OR upper(g.grade_value) IN ('F','FAIL','NP','U')
        THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
    ) AS failures
  FROM public.courses c
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  LEFT JOIN public.grades g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
)
SELECT
  course_id,
  course_code,
  course_name,
  failures,
  total_graded AS total_enrollments,
  CASE WHEN total_graded = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ROUND(failures::numeric / total_graded, 4) END AS failure_rate
FROM course_stats
ORDER BY failure_rate DESC, course_code ASC;
assumptions: Failure is defined as numeric grade_value < 3.0 on the scale present in grades.grade_value, or letter-like failing values (F, FAIL, NP, U).
Failure rate denominator is the count of enrollments with a recorded grade (grades.id present); enrollments without a grade are excluded.
All course sections and academic years are included (no additional semester/year filtering).
grades.grade_value stores either numeric strings (e.g., '4.5') or letter codes; the query detects numeric strings with a regex before casting.

payload.sql:
WITH course_stats AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.code AS course_code,
    c.name AS course_name,
    COUNT(g.id) FILTER (WHERE g.id IS NOT NULL) AS total_graded,
    SUM(
      CASE
        WHEN (
               g.grade_value ~ '^[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?$'
               AND g.grade_value::numeric < 3.0
             )
             OR upper(g.grade_value) IN ('F','FAIL','NP','U')
        THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
    ) AS failures
  FROM public.courses c
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  LEFT JOIN public.grades g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
)
SELECT
  course_id,
  course_code,
  course_name,
  failures,
  total_graded AS total_enrollments,
  CASE WHEN total_graded = 0 THEN 0 ELSE ROUND(failures::numeric / total_graded, 4) END AS failure_rate
FROM course_stats
ORDER BY failure_rate DESC, course_code ASC;
